Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
Apil3,2006
Joe E. Quiroz
President, Board of Trustees
Paso Robles Public Schools
P.O. Box 7010
Paso Robles, CA 93447
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-047
Dear Mr. Quiroz:
This letter addresses your request for written advice regarding Ms. Jeanne Hunt
owner of Children's Garden/Templeton School District Board on the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the o'Act").I After a careful review of the facts you have submitted,
we decline to provide written advice at this time. Your questions involve analysis of
facts which have already occurred, such that issuing written advice at this time would
relate to past conduct. Unless Ms. Jeanne Hunt has authorized you as her representative,
and request involves prospective conduct the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot
address your questions or provide an analysis. (Regulation 18329(b)(8XA) and (cX4)tAr,
copy enclosed.)
Should you have any additional questions on this matter, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
John W. Wallace
stant General Counsel
Division

I Government Code sections 8 1 000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
-SE,

sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission declines to provide written advice at this time because the questions involve analysis of facts which have already occurred (past conduct). Unless Ms. Jeanne Hunt has authorized the requester as her representative and the request involves prospective conduct, the Commission cannot address the questions or provide analysis.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Ms. Jeanne Hunt, owner of Children's Garden, violated provisions of the Political Reform Act regarding her service on the Templeton School District Board.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue written advice because the request concerns past conduct rather than prospective conduct, and was not submitted with authorization from the interested party.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (W-06-047). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the question and conclusion were extracted from the body text. The document references Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (c)(4) as the basis for declination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Ms. Jeanne Hunt, owner of Children's Garden, violated provisions of the Political Reform Act regarding her service on the Templeton School District Board.

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission declines to provide written advice at this time because the questions involve analysis of facts which have already occurred (past conduct). Unless Ms. Jeanne Hunt has authorized the requester as her representative and the request involves prospective conduct, the Commission cannot address the questions or provide analysis.

Unless Ms. Jeanne Hunt has authorized you as her representative, and request involves prospective conduct the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide an analysis. embedding.summary: On April 3, 2006, the FPPC declined to provide written advice regarding whether Ms. Jeanne Hunt's ownership interest in Children's Garden created a conflict of interest with her service on the Templeton School District Board, as the questions involved analysis of already-occurred facts rather than prospective conduct.
